# File Classification: 3-Tier System

Classify every file in the generated project into one of three tiers to determine how template updates are applied.

## Table of Contents

- [Tier Definitions](#tier-definitions)
- [Tier 1 — Template-Managed Files](#tier-1--template-managed)
- [Tier 2 — Merge-Required Files](#tier-2--merge-required)
- [Tier 3 — Local-Owned Files](#tier-3--local-owned)
- [Dynamic Reclassification](#dynamic-reclassification)
- [Tier 2 Merge Strategies](#tier-2-merge-strategies)

---

## Tier Definitions

| Tier | Policy | Conflict Strategy |
|------|--------|-------------------|
| **1 — Template-managed** | Accept template version unconditionally | Apply `.rej` / accept update |
| **2 — Merge-required** | Accept template structural changes, preserve local additions | Intelligent merge |
| **3 — Local-owned** | Never overwrite with template content | Discard `.rej` / restore from git |

---

## Tier 1 — Template-Managed

Files where the template is the source of truth. Local edits are rare and should not survive updates.

```text
.copier-answers.yml          # Always regenerated by copier
.editorconfig
.gitignore
.pre-commit-config.yaml
.readthedocs.yml
.git-cliff.toml
LICENSE
CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
CONTRIBUTING.md
docs/api-submodule.html
docs/assets/.gitkeep
docs/assets/favicon.png
docs/assets/logo.png
docs/assets/logo_dark.png
docs/assets/logo_light.png
docs/assets/made_by_stateful-y.png
docs/assets/README.md
docs/hooks.py
docs/javascripts/mathjax.js
docs/javascripts/readthedocs.js
docs/material/overrides/api-index.html
docs/material/overrides/api-page.html
docs/material/overrides/api-submodule.html
docs/material/overrides/main.html
docs/stylesheets/gallery.css       # conditional: include_examples
docs/stylesheets/theme.css
.github/dependabot.yml
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/bug_report.yml
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml
.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/feature_request.yml
.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md
.github/skills/**                   # Skill files managed by template
```

---

## Tier 2 — Merge-Required

Files commonly customized by project owners but that also receive meaningful template updates. Both template changes AND local additions must be preserved.

```text
README.md
CHANGELOG.md
pyproject.toml
mkdocs.yml
noxfile.py
justfile
docs/index.md
docs/pages/reference/api.md
docs/pages/how-to/contribute.md
.github/workflows/tests.yml        # conditional: include_actions
.github/workflows/pr-title.yml     # conditional: include_actions
.github/workflows/publish-release.yml  # conditional: include_actions
.github/workflows/nightly.yml      # conditional: include_actions
.github/workflows/changelog.yml    # conditional: include_actions
```

---

## Tier 3 — Local-Owned

Project-specific files where the template only provides initial scaffolding. Never overwrite with template content after initial generation.

```text
src/<package_name>/**              # All source code
tests/**                           # All test files
examples/**                        # conditional: include_examples
docs/pages/explanation/concepts.md
docs/pages/tutorials/getting-started.md
docs/pages/tutorials/examples.md    # conditional: include_examples
docs/examples/**                   # conditional: include_examples
```

**Also Tier 3**: Any file that exists in the project but has NO corresponding template source (custom files added by the project).

---

## Dynamic Reclassification

The baseline diff (Step 2 of the workflow) can promote or demote files:

- **Tier 2 → effectively Tier 1**: If a Tier 2 file is identical to the baseline (user never customized it), treat it as Tier 1 during this update — accept template changes without merge.
- **Tier 1 → Tier 2**: If a normally Tier 1 file has local edits (e.g., custom `.gitignore` entries), treat it as Tier 2 — merge instead of overwriting.

This ensures the skill only spends effort merging files that actually have local customizations.

---

## Tier 2 Merge Strategies

### README.md

- **Accept from template**: Badge format changes, structural improvements (section ordering, formatting)
- **Preserve local**: Project description, features list, custom sections (any heading not in the template), custom badges, acknowledgements content
- **Strategy**: Match sections by `## Heading`. Replace template headings with template content. Keep headings not present in the template. Preserve content under shared headings that differs from the baseline (e.g., customized "Features" list).

### pyproject.toml

- **Accept from template**: `[build-system]`, `[tool.*]` sections (ruff, hatch, pytest, coverage config), dependency-group version bumps for `tests`, `lint`, `docs`, `fix`, `examples`
- **Preserve local**: `[project.dependencies]` additions beyond template defaults, custom `[dependency-groups]` entries, custom `[project.scripts]`, custom `[project.entry-points]`, `[project.optional-dependencies]`
- **Strategy**: Parse by TOML sections. For each section, determine if it's template-owned or locally-added. Template-owned sections get updated. Locally-added sections are preserved. Within shared sections like `[project]`, keep local additions (extra classifiers, URLs, etc.).

### mkdocs.yml

- **Accept from template**: Theme configuration, plugin list, markdown extensions, extra CSS/JS references
- **Preserve local**: Custom `nav` entries (pages added beyond template defaults), extra plugins, custom theme overrides
- **Strategy**: Parse by top-level YAML keys. Template-owned keys get updated. Local additions (new nav items, extra watch paths) are preserved. The `nav` key requires special attention — keep custom entries that don't exist in the template nav.

### noxfile.py

- **Accept from template**: Template-defined sessions (lint, tests, docs, etc.), import changes, decorator changes
- **Preserve local**: Custom nox sessions (functions not in the template noxfile)
- **Strategy**: Identify functions by `@nox.session` decorator and function name. Update functions present in the template. Preserve functions not in the template.

### justfile

- **Accept from template**: Template-defined recipes, variable updates
- **Preserve local**: Custom recipes not in the template
- **Strategy**: Identify recipes by name. Update template recipes. Preserve custom recipes.

### .github/workflows/*.yml

- **Accept from template**: Updates to existing jobs (new steps, version bumps, matrix changes)
- **Preserve local**: Entirely new jobs added to existing workflows, new workflow files not from the template, custom steps appended to existing jobs
- **Strategy**: For each workflow file from the template, update template-defined jobs. Preserve locally-added jobs (job names not in the template). For custom steps within shared jobs, append local steps after template steps. Entirely new workflow files (not from template) are always Tier 3.

### CHANGELOG.md

- **Accept from template**: Format/header changes only
- **Preserve local**: All changelog entries
- **Strategy**: Keep the local CHANGELOG.md content. Only update the header/format if the template changed it. Never discard changelog entries.

### docs/index.md, docs/pages/how-to/contribute.md, docs/pages/reference/api.md

- **Accept from template**: Structural/format changes, new template sections
- **Preserve local**: Custom content within existing sections, additional sections
- **Strategy**: Same as README.md — match by heading, merge per-section.
